choice: Every Account links to an owner Person and carries an ownership type {individual, joint}. Tax-deferred and Roth accounts (d424a37f) are ALWAYS individually owned — the IRS has no joint retirement accounts — so RMDs (fe56f1d6) compute against that single owner's age. Allow 'joint' only for taxable accounts, owned by both adults; for engine purposes a joint taxable account behaves like an individual one (no per-owner tax rule differs at v1's bracketed-net fidelity, dc489f83), so 'joint' is mainly a display/ownership-transfer affordance.
rationale: d410eb00 placed RMD applicability and ownership on the Account container but left the actual ownership cardinality unpinned. RMDs are statutorily per-owner-age, so a tax-deferred Account must resolve to exactly one owner Person; making that a hard constraint prevents an undefined 'whose age?' at RMD time. Joint taxable ownership is the common real household case and matters for the death-transition question below, but adds no tax math at v1 fidelity.
